reasoning: Following the intense military escalation in May 2025 (Operation Sindoor), a US-brokered ceasefire was established on May 10. While there were immediate minor violations, recent reports (as of May 21) indicate the ceasefire 'has held; fragile, uneasy but still unbroken.' The involvement of high-level US mediation, including plans for talks at a neutral site, introduces strong diplomatic pressure to maintain the pause in hostilities over the short term. Because the initial retaliatory aims for the April Pahalgam attack were largely met by the May 7 strikes, an unprovoked resumption of kinetic strikes hitting 'Pakistan proper' (Punjab, Sindh, KPK, Baluchistan) specifically in June is unlikely unless triggered by a new major terrorist event. Some risk remains due to the proximity of the International Border to Punjab and India's stated 'new normal' doctrine, placing the probability at around 16%.
